description: ML model assessing consumer loan applications and generating a creditworthiness score and decision recommendation
primary_purpose: Credit risk assessment and loan decisioning
data_inputs: Credit bureau data; bank transaction history; employment status; income; postcode; existing debt obligations
notes: Annex III Category 5(b) — creditworthiness assessment of natural persons. Output is the primary input to lending decisions, not advisory. Postcode and employment fields carry known proxy-discrimination risk. Explainability layer exists but lacks documented validation against Article 13 transparency standard.

description: NLP-based resume parsing and candidate ranking tool used in early-stage recruitment screening
primary_purpose: Recruitment — CV screening and candidate shortlisting
data_inputs: CV text and attachments; cover letters; structured application form data; years of experience
outputs: Ranked candidate shortlist; suitability score; auto-rejection flag below threshold
notes: Annex III Category 4(a) — recruitment and selection screening. No documented conformity assessment since go-live. No bias audit conducted. Auto-rejection below score threshold occurs without mandatory human review — this is a substantive gap against Article 14. Highest-priority remediation item in the portfolio.

description: Transaction monitoring system using anomaly detection to flag potentially suspicious activity for AML/CTF compliance
data_inputs: Transaction amounts and patterns; counterparty data; customer risk rating; geographic indicators; historical alert outcomes
outputs: Suspicious activity alert with risk score; case routed to financial crime analyst queue
affected_persons: All HFG account holders and counterparties
notes: Annex III Category 5(a) interpretation applies given use in creditworthiness-adjacent risk scoring context for financial service access; treated as high-risk on a conservative legal reading pending final counsel confirmation. Human analyst reviews every alert before SAR filing — human-in-the-loop is substantive. Vendor model retraining cadence not contractually disclosed to HFG; transparency gap with vendor.

description: Voice biometric system used to authenticate customers calling into the contact centre, replacing manual security questions
primary_purpose: Biometric identity verification for customer authentication
data_inputs: Voice recordings; enrolled voiceprint templates; call metadata
notes: Biometric categorisation under Annex III Category 1 — system uses biometric data for identification/verification of natural persons. No documented data protection impact assessment specific to biometric processing under Article 10. No fallback authentication path audit. Enrolment consent flow has not been reviewed by legal since launch. Critical documentation gap.

description: Conversational AI chatbot handling Tier-1 customer queries on web and mobile banking channels
primary_purpose: Customer service automation and query deflection
data_inputs: Customer query text; account metadata (with consent); product FAQ knowledge base
outputs: Natural language response; intent classification; escalation to human agent on low confidence
notes: Article 52(1) transparency obligation applies — disclosure that users are interacting with an AI system is implemented on web channel via persistent banner. Mobile app disclosure implemented at session start only, not persistently visible; partial compliance gap.

description: Generative AI tool producing synthetic marketing copy and image variants for campaign A/B testing
data_inputs: Campaign briefs; brand style guides; historical campaign performance data; stock image libraries
outputs: Generated ad copy variants; AI-generated image assets for testing
notes: Article 50 transparency obligation for AI-generated/synthetic content applies — generated image assets must be marked as artificially generated where they could be mistaken for authentic content. Labelling is not currently applied consistently across all output channels. Compliance gap flagged to Marketing Ops.

description: Workplace analytics tool tracking employee productivity signals (system usage, task completion patterns) for management reporting
data_inputs: System login/logout times; application usage patterns; task completion timestamps; team-level aggregation
outputs: Productivity dashboards; manager-level team analytics; individual-level flags for unusually low activity
notes: Not an Annex III category, but classification was reviewed carefully given employment-context sensitivity — individual-level flagging (rather than team-aggregate only) brings this closer to a workplace-monitoring risk profile that Works Councils in DE have specifically queried. Employee notice of monitoring exists but consultation with DE Works Council on individual-level flag feature is incomplete. Flagged for HR/Legal follow-up.

description: Internal business intelligence and forecasting tool used by Finance for portfolio performance trend analysis
data_inputs: Aggregated, anonymised loan book and portfolio performance data; macroeconomic indicators
outputs: Trend forecasts; portfolio risk dashboards for internal management reporting
affected_persons: Internal management only — no external natural persons affected directly
notes: No Annex III category applies. Output is internal-facing only and does not affect any individual customer or employee directly. Data inputs are aggregated and anonymised prior to model use. No specific EU AI Act obligations identified.

description: NLP-based regulatory horizon-scanning tool monitoring EU and national regulator publications for relevance to HFG's business
data_inputs: Public regulatory texts; EBA, ECB, and national regulator publications; press releases
outputs: Relevance-tagged summary digest; alert notifications routed to compliance officers
notes: Internal-use system with no direct or indirect impact on natural persons outside HFG. All outputs are validated by qualified compliance staff before any action is taken. Low risk profile confirmed; no remediation required.

description: AI-assisted scheduling tool generating recommended shift rosters for branch and contact centre staff
data_inputs: Employee availability; skill/qualification tags; historical demand patterns; labour cost constraints
outputs: Recommended shift roster; flagged scheduling conflicts for manager review
affected_persons: HFG branch and contact centre employees
notes: No Annex III category applies — system optimises scheduling logistics and does not evaluate, score, or rank individual employees. All recommended rosters are subject to manager approval before publication. Risk tier to be reconfirmed if scope expands to include individual performance-linked scheduling decisions.
